name: Repository Scaffold & Root AI-Legible Entry Points

files_create.purpose: Stub index for the standard/ module (compliant frontmatter only; full content is feature 002).
Stub index for the methodology/ module (compliant frontmatter only; full content is feature 003).
Stub index for the registry/ module (compliant frontmatter only; full content is feature 006).
Stub index for the templates/ module (compliant frontmatter only; full content is feature 004).
Stub index for the schema/ module (compliant frontmatter only; full content is feature 001).
Stub index for the tools/ module (compliant frontmatter only; full content is feature 005).
Root token-efficient architecture + execution-command summary; YAML frontmatter with 6 required keys; includes '## Out of Scope' section.
Root human-readable overview and quick-start entry point.
Semantic version history file seeded with an 'Unreleased' section only (v0.1.0 entry belongs to feature 007).
Standard ignore patterns for the repository.

non_goals: No module content beyond the six README stubs (schema files are feature 001)
No standard/ protocol content (NDS.md, GIP.md, AGF.md, anti_hallucination.md are feature 002)
No methodology/ protocol docs (00_planning.md through 05_release_train.md are feature 003)
No templates/ content beyond the module README (feature 004)
No tools/ skill payload content (skill.json, SKILL.md, interface.md are feature 005)
No registry/ content beyond the module README, and no root NIZAM.json (feature 006)
No CHANGELOG.md v0.1.0 entry (feature 007 seeds the first real release entry)
No NizamIQ branding or infrastructure endpoints anywhere in shipped content

verification.acceptance_test: The six module directories exist: test -d standard && test -d methodology && test -d registry && test -d templates && test -d schema && test -d tools
verification.command: test -d standard && test -d methodology && test -d registry && test -d templates && test -d schema && test -d tools && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: Root files exist: test -f CONTEXT.md && test -f README.md && test -f CHANGELOG.md && test -f .gitignore
verification.command: test -f CONTEXT.md && test -f README.md && test -f CHANGELOG.md && test -f .gitignore && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: CONTEXT.md contains an '## Out of Scope' section (grep -q '## Out of Scope' CONTEXT.md)
verification.command: grep -q '## Out of Scope' CONTEXT.md && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: CONTEXT.md begins with YAML frontmatter (head -1 CONTEXT.md equals '---')
The six module README stub files exist: standard/README.md, methodology/README.md, registry/README.md, templates/README.md, schema/README.md, tools/README.md
verification.command: test -f standard/README.md && test -f methodology/README.md && test -f registry/README.md && test -f templates/README.md && test -f schema/README.md && test -f tools/README.md && echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: No shipped file references a NizamIQ infrastructure endpoint (grep -ri 'nizamiq' CONTEXT.md README.md returns nothing)
verification.command: test -f CONTEXT.md && test -f README.md && ! grep -riq 'nizamiq' CONTEXT.md README.md && echo PASS

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/000-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/000-verify-01.txt for the first entry, 000-verify-02.txt for the second, ..., 000-verify-06.txt for the sixth). One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
